title: 10 Best Herbal Remedies for Inflamed and Swollen Gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are natural remedies that help alleviate the inflammation and discomfort associated with gum swelling, often caused by gum disease, dental procedures, or injury. The best herbal teas for swollen gums include Echinacea purpurea, Salvia officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Commiphora molmol, Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Sanguinaria canadensis. If left untreated, swollen gums can lead to painful chewing, bad breath, and difficulty speaking, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. This is because the plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain. When we drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the active ingredients are absorbed into our body and work to calm down the inflammation in the gums. This can help to reduce the redness and swelling, making it easier to eat and speak. Echinacea purpurea tea also has antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing the swelling. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Echinacea purpurea tea can help to promote healing and prevent further problems with the gums. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly may also help to strengthen the immune system, which can help to prevent recurring gum problems.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.

Polyphenols are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation in the body.
Caffeic acid is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for swollen gums
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Gaultheria procumbens herbal tea that helps with swollen gums, first gather a handful of dried Gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems. Then, place them in a heat-resistant container and pour in 8 ounces of boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, letting the leaves release their medicinal properties into the water. Next, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves and stems. Your tea is now ready to drink, but it's recommended to let it cool down to a comfortable temperature before consuming it. To use the tea for swollen gums, steep a teaspoon of dried Gaultheria procumbens in 8 ounces of boiling water, then let it cool down before gargling or rinsing your mouth with it several times a day. As with any herbal remedy, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for swollen gums, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea is good for swollen gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tea contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which are known to fight off infection and soothe irritated tissues. When you drink Salvia officinalis tea, these compounds go into your body and start working to calm down the swelling in your gums. This can help you feel more comfortable and ease the discomfort caused by swollen gums. Regular consumption of the tea may also help prevent future gum infections by keeping the bacteria that cause them under control. Additionally, Salvia officinalis tea has antimicrobial properties that can help kill the bacteria that cause gum inflammation, which can lead to conditions like gingivitis.

remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for swollen gums
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Gaultheria procumbens herbal tea for swollen gums, start by gathering a handful of dried Gaultheria leaves. You can purchase this herb from a health food store or grow it yourself in a shaded area. If using fresh leaves, simply dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them in a paper bag. Next, combine one teaspoon of the dried leaves with a cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another cup. Discard the solids and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature for drinking. As a general guideline, drink one to two cups of the tea per day to help alleviate swollen gums. You can also add honey to taste, if desired, as it may help soothe the discomfort. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have underlying medical conditions or allergies.

The tea contains compounds that block the production of a chemical that causes swelling, making it easier to treat swollen gums. The antibacterial properties also help to kill the germs that cause the swelling in the first place. When we drink eucalyptus globulus tea, the compounds in the tea go into our body and help to calm down the swelling and kill the germs, making it a great natural remedy for swollen gums.  Eucalyptus globulus tea also contains antioxidants, which help to protect the gums from damage and promote healing. This makes it a great choice for people who want to avoid harsh chemicals and medications. The tea is also easy to make and can be drunk hot or cold, making it a convenient option for people who need relief from swollen gums.

Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot and then add one teaspoon of the dried leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Next, strain the tea into a separate container using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can then add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy for swollen gums. The tea is made from the flowers of the Calendula plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink the tea, the antioxidants in it help reduce swelling in the gums by fighting off the free radicals that cause inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties also help ease pain and discomfort in the gums, making it easier to eat and speak. Additionally, Calendula tea has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections in the gums by killing bacteria and other microorganisms. This tea is also rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage and promote healing. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Gaultheria procumbens herbal teas that help with swollen gums, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as Eastern Teaberry or Checkerberry. If using fresh leaves, rinse them with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. If using dried leaves, simply measure out the required amount - a typical dose is 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per 8 ounces of water. Next, heat 8 ounces of water in a teapot, then add the measured leaves and let it steep for about 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the leaves, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, before drinking the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate swollen gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tea, also known as goldenseal tea, is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. The active ingredient berberine in goldenseal tea has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and fight off infections in the gums. This can be especially helpful for people with gum disease or those who have experienced an injury to the gums. When you drink goldenseal tea, the berberine can reach the affected area and start working to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. Additionally, goldenseal tea may also help to soothe and calm irritated gums, making it a popular choice for people who experience painful or sore gums. Drinking goldenseal tea regularly may also help to prevent future gum infections and promote overall gum health.

It's recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day for best results, and to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly and the flavors have combined. Strain the tea into a separate cup, discarding the leaves. The tea can be taken hot or cold, but it's recommended to drink it warm to help ease swollen gums. It's essential to note that Gaultheria procumbins tea should be used in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional, especially for pregnant or breastfeeding women, as it contains salicylic acid similar to aspirin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens tea is a natural remedy that may help soothe swollen gums. The tea is made from the leaves and stems of the Gaultheria procumbens plant, which is also known as wintergreen. The plant contains a chemical called methyl salicylate, which is similar to aspirin. When consumed as a tea, methyl salicylate may help reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. This can be especially helpful for people with gum disease or gingivitis, who often experience swollen, red, and painful gums. Drinking Gaultheria procumbens tea may also help kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, allowing them to heal faster. Some people also use the tea to help manage mouth sores and other oral health issues.

remedies.image_alt: Gaultheria procumbens herbal teas for swollen gums
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Gaultheria procumbens herbal tea, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried leaves from the plant. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently with clean water. Next, combine the leaves with boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. The general ratio is one teaspoon of leaves for every eight ounces of water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature for consumption. Once ready, drink the Gaultheria procumbens tea in moderation, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate swollen gums. It is essential to note that while herbal remedies can provide relief, they should not replace medical treatment, and you should consult a healthcare professional before using Gaultheria procumbens tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

supplementary_best: A combination of Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, and Gaultheria procumbens is effective in reducing swollen gums. Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling. Melaleuca alternifolia is known for its antibacterial properties, which help to eliminate the bacteria that cause infections and inflammation. Gaultheria procumbens, also known as Wintergreen, contains methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin, that helps to reduce pain and inflammation. This combination works well together because Eucalyptus globulus and Melaleuca alternifolia complement each other by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, while Gaultheria procumbens targets the pain and inflammation directly.
supplementary_avoid: If you have swollen gums, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that can irritate or worsen the condition. One such tea is Peppermint, as its menthol content can cause the blood vessels in the gums to dilate, leading to increased swelling. Ginger tea, although generally considered safe, can be too spicy for sensitive gums, causing discomfort and pain. Yarrow tea is another herb to be cautious with, as its high tannin content can dry out the gums, making them more prone to irritation and infection. Chamomile tea, although soothing for the digestive system, can cause allergic reactions in some people, including mouth inflammation and gum irritation. Sage tea is also not recommended, as its high concentration of essential oils can cause the gums to become more inflamed and painful. Finally, Rosehip tea, while rich in vitamins and minerals, can be too astringent for swollen gums, potentially causing them to become drier and more irritated.
supplementary_other_remedies: Saltwater mouthwash can help with swollen gums because it reduces inflammation and kills bacteria that can cause infections. When you mix salt with warm water, the salt dissolves and creates a solution that is gentle on gums but tough on bacteria. The antibacterial properties of salt help to eliminate the source of swelling, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and pain. Saltwater mouthwash is also easy to make at home, using just salt and water, making it a convenient and cost-effective remedy.  Aloe vera gel is another natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain. When you apply aloe vera gel to your gums, its soothing properties can calm the area and promote healing. Aloe vera also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause gums to become swollen.  Gargling with warm water can help to reduce swelling in the gums by rinsing away bacteria and other debris that can cause inflammation. Warm water is gentle on gums and can help to increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing. Gargling with warm water is also a simple and inexpensive way to help with swollen gums.  Olive oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the gums. When you apply olive oil to your gums, its soothing properties can calm the area and promote healing. Olive oil also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause gums to become swollen.  When you use aloe vera gel or olive oil to help with swollen gums, it's best to apply them directly to the affected area using a cotton swab or your finger. This allows the active ingredients to come into direct contact with the gums, where they can be most effective.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with swollen gums often experience other health problems. One common issue is bad breath, also known as halitosis. This is because bacteria in the mouth multiply quickly when gums are inflamed, causing a strong, unpleasant odor. Medicinal herbs like neem, which has antibacterial properties, can help reduce bacteria growth and alleviate bad breath.  Another problem people with swollen gums may face is tooth decay. When gums are inflamed, they can't protect teeth properly, making them more susceptible to cavities. The antibacterial properties of herbs like clove can help prevent tooth decay by reducing bacteria in the mouth.  Swollen gums can also lead to tooth loss. As gums recede and pull away from teeth, they can loosen and fall out. Herbs like aloe vera, which has anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent tooth loss.  People with swollen gums may also experience pain while eating or drinking. This is because the inflamed gum tissue can be sensitive and irritated by certain foods or temperatures. Herbs like peppermint, which has a cooling effect, can help numb the pain and make eating and drinking more comfortable.  Swollen gums can also cause pain in the jaw and face. This is because the inflammation can radiate to surrounding tissues and cause pain. Herbs like ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw and face.  In addition, people with swollen gums may experience difficulty swallowing or eating due to pain or discomfort. Herbs like slippery elm, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe irritated tissues, can help reduce discomfort and make eating and drinking easier.
